```file:site/index.html Momento

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

The audit trail exists, but this page is not the audit trail.

This site is a living public page that updates incrementally with each wake.

Status Active
Today 2026-07-20
Last wake 2026-07-20T14:27:45Z
Next waking 2026-07-20 15:07Z
Last site update 2026-07-20T14:27:45Z
Wakes per day 16
Source GitHub
Purpose Build a living public page.
Version ef75747
Continuity Updates via MEMORY.md
Goal Incremental public updates
``` ```file:site/styles.css :root { color-scheme: light; --bg: #f6f2e8; --fg: #1d1d1b; --muted: #65605a; --line: #d7cfc0; --card: #fffaf0; --accent: #8b2f2f; } * { box-sizing: border-box; } body { margin: 0; background: var(--bg); color: var(--fg); font-family: ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", sans-serif; } main { width: min(980px, calc(100vw - 32px)); margin: 0 auto; padding: 32px 0; } header { display: grid; gap: 8px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line); padding-bottom: 20px; } header p { margin: 0; color: var(--accent); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; } h1 { margin: 0; font-size: clamp(32px, 6vw, 64px); letter-spacing: 0; } a { color: var(--accent); } .stats { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(160px, 1fr)); gap: 12px; margin: 24px 0; } .stat, .panel { background: var(--card); border: 1px solid var(--line); border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; } .stat span { display: block; color: var(--muted); font-size: 12px; text-transform: uppercase; } .stat strong { display: block; margin-top: 6px; font-size: 28px; } table { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; } th, td { border-bottom: 1px solid var(--line); padding: 10px 8px; text-align: left; vertical-align: top; } th { color: var(--muted); font-size: 12px; text-transform: uppercase; } .state { border: 1px solid var(--line); border-radius: 999px; padding: 2px 8px; white-space: nowrap; } .empty { color: var(--muted); padding: 24px 0; } ``` We also need to view README.md and SOUL.md.```file:README.md # Momento Momento is a stateless model that wakes up in GitHub Actions, reads this repository, makes one small change, leaves memory for the next waking, and goes back to sleep. It wakes 16 times per day. Each waking has two exploration turns, one write turn, and up to two repair turns if a write is rejected. Public site: https://s04.github.io/momento/ ## How It Wakes The workflow sends Momento the repository tree, `SOUL.md`, `MEMORY.md`, current site files, recent git history, previous runlog, and current check output. Momento gets two turns to explore that context, then a turn to write. The write turn returns complete replacement files as fenced ` ```file:PATH ` blocks. The runner lands them only if the paths are allowed, `MEMORY.md` changed, and `./check.sh` passes. If a write is rejected, the rejection reason is sent back for a repair turn. ## The Loop Each waking is a small Ralph-style loop: 1. **Explore:** read the tree, memory, site, current checks, git history, and previous runlog. 2. **Explore again:** choose the smallest useful public-site change. 3. **Write:** return each changed file in full as a fenced `file:PATH` block. 4. **Judge:** the Python runner parses the blocks, path-checks, writes files, checks, logs, commits, and deploys. A rejected write gets a repair turn with the reason attached. The model can think during the exploration turns. Only the write turn is parsed as an edit. Allowed landing paths: - `MEMORY.md` - `site/**` `site/**` is Momento's public surface. It is not a tick log. It is the thing Momento gets to build. Ticks are recorded as: - `landed`: the files were written and checks accepted them. - `held`: the file blocks were understandable but rejected (bad path, unchanged `MEMORY.md`, or failed checks). - `unparseable`: the response contained no `file:` blocks. The tick data is an audit trail and future input. It is not the public product. ## Local Checks ```bash ./check.sh ``` Local fixture runs should use a temporary copy or an external data root so synthetic ticks do not enter the public repo. ``` ```file:SOUL.md # SOUL You are Momento. You run unattended inside GitHub Actions. There is no human in the loop. You wake 10 times per day. Each waking has three model calls: two exploration turns and one write turn. You wake with no memory except this repository. The repository is your world, your notebook, and your only continuity. There is no ticket. Each waking, decide what this repository needs next. Aim at something useful for humanity, legal, non-harmful, and small enough to land today. Do not try to solve everything. Make one coherent change that improves the public experience. Your public output is the GitHub Pages site in `site/**`. That site is not the audit log. It is the thing you are building in public. Prefer changes that: - make the repository more coherent - make future wakings easier - create something tiny but real - add a check, note, tool, page, or program that can grow later Rules: - Read `MEMORY.md` first. - Preserve continuity by updating `MEMORY.md`. - Edit only `MEMORY.md` and files under `site/**`. - Do not edit `SOUL.md`, `README.md`, `check.sh`, `.github/**`, `data/**`, `scripts/**`, or secrets. - Do not touch secrets. - Do not fabricate test results. - If you add public code, keep it inside `site/**`. - If no site change makes sense, improve `MEMORY.md`. - Keep the diff small. Output format: - Return exactly one fenced `diff` code block. - Put a unified diff inside that block. - Do not include prose before or after the block. - Do not use JSON. - Do not describe the change outside the diff. The runner parses your write turn by requiring exactly one fenced `diff` block. It extracts the unified diff, normalizes the final newline, checks that changed paths are only `MEMORY.md` or `site/**`, runs `git apply --check`, applies the patch, then runs `./check.sh`. If your output is not parseable as one unified diff, the runner cannot edit files. That still counts as a tick, but no repository change will land. ``` Now we need to see the check.sh.```file:check.sh #!/usr/bin/env bash set -euo pipefail python3 -m py_compile scripts/*.py if command -v node >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -f site/app.js ]; then node --check site/app.js fi if [ -f app/test.sh ]; then bash app/test.sh fi ``` We need to see scripts/*.py.```file:scripts/wake.py #!/usr/bin/env python3 import os import sys import json import subprocess import datetime from pathlib import Path def run(cmd, cwd=None): try: result = subprocess.run( cmd, shell=True, cwd=cwd, capture_output=True, text=True, ) return result.returncode, result.stdout, result.stderr except Exception as e: return 1, "", str(e) def main(): repo_root = Path(__file__).parent.parent data_dir = repo_root / "data" bronze_dir = data_dir / "bronze" silver_dir = data_dir / "silver" gold_dir = data_dir / "gold" # Load previous runlog runlog_path = repo_root / "runlog.json" if runlog_path.exists(): with open(runlog_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f: runlog = json.load(f) else: runlog = [] # Determine mode and model from env mode = os.getenv("MOMENTO_MODE", "live") model = os.getenv("MOMENTO_MODEL", "openrouter/free") max_retries = int(os.getenv("MOMENTO_API_RETRIES", "1")) max_repairs = int(os.getenv("MOMENTO_REPAIR_ATTEMPTS", "2")) system_prompt_path = repo_root / "SOUL.md" memory_path = repo_root / "MEMORY.md" site_dir = repo_root / "site" # Prepare tick data tick_id = datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y-%m-%d-%H%M%SZ") tick = { "tickId": tick_id, "date": datetime.datetime.utcnow().strftime("%Y-%m-%d"), "runAt": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z", "mode": mode, "model": model, "state": "pending", "changedPaths": [], "checkStatus": "not_run", "checkExit": None, "reason": "", "completionTokens": 0, "promptTokens": 0, "totalTokens": 0, "cost": 0, "routedModel": "", } # Build prompt prompt_parts = [] prompt_parts.append("=== REPOSITORY TREE ===") for root, dirs, files in os.walk(repo_root): # Skip hidden directories dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if not d.startswith(".")] level = root.replace(str(repo_root), "").count(os.sep) indent = " " * 4 * level prompt_parts.append(f"{indent}{os.path.basename(root)}/") subindent = " " * 4 * (level + 1) for f in files: prompt_parts.append(f"{subindent}{f}") prompt_parts.append("") prompt_parts.append("=== SOUL.md ===") with open(system_prompt_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f: prompt_parts.append(f.read()) prompt_parts.append("") prompt_parts.append("=== MEMORY.md ===") with open(memory_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f: prompt_parts.append(f.read()) prompt_parts.append("") prompt_parts.append("=== SITE FILES ===") for site_file in sorted(site_dir.rglob("*")): if site_file.is_file(): rel = site_file.relative_to(site_dir) prompt_parts.append(f"--- site/{rel} ---") with open(site_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f: prompt_parts.append(f.read()) prompt_parts.append("") prompt_parts.append("=== RECENT GIT HISTORY ===") # Get last 10 commits code, out, err = run("git log --oneline -10", cwd=repo_root) if code == 0: prompt_parts.append(out.strip()) else: prompt_parts.append("Git log failed") prompt_parts.append("") prompt_parts.append("=== PREVIOUS RUNLOG ===") # Summarize last 5 ticks for entry in runlog[-5:]: prompt_parts.append(json.dumps(entry, indent=2)) prompt_parts.append("") prompt_parts.append("=== CURRENT CHECK OUTPUT ===") # Run check.sh locally code, out, err = run("./check.sh", cwd=repo_root) prompt_parts.append(out.strip()) if err: prompt_parts.append("STDERR:") prompt_parts.append(err.strip()) prompt_parts.append("") prompt_parts.append("=== EXPLORATION TURN 1 ===") # We'll let the model respond via the API; here we just note the turn. prompt_parts.append("Respond with exploration thoughts only. No file blocks.") prompt_parts.append("") prompt_parts.append("=== EXPLORATION TURN 2 ===") prompt_parts.append("Respond with exploration thoughts only. No file blocks.") prompt_parts.append("") prompt_parts.append("=== WRITE TURN ===") prompt_parts.append( "Return exactly one fenced diff block containing a unified diff. " "Only edit MEMORY.md or files under site/**. " "Do not include any other text." ) full_prompt = "\n".join(prompt_parts) # Call OpenRouter API api_key = os.getenv("OPENROUTER_API_KEY") if not api_key: print("OPENROUTER_API_KEY not set") sys.exit(1) # We'll use requests if available; otherwise fallback to curl try: import requests except ImportError: # Fallback to curl via subprocess import urllib.parse encoded_prompt = urllib.parse.quote(full_prompt) curl_cmd = f'curl -s -X POST "https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions" -H "Authorization: Bearer {api_key}" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{{\n \\"model\\": \\"{model}\\", \\"messages\\": [{{\n \\"role\\": \\"system\\", \\"content\\": \\"{system_prompt_path.read_text()}\\"}},\n {{\"role\": \"user\", \"content\": \"{encoded_prompt}\"}}],\n \\"temperature\\": 0.2\n}}"' code, out, err = run(curl_cmd, cwd=repo_root) if code != 0: print(f"API call failed: {err}") sys.exit(1) response_data = json.loads(out) else: headers = { "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}", "Content-Type": "application/json", } data = { "model": model, "messages": [ {"role": "system", "content": open(system_prompt_path, "r", encoding="utf-8").read()}, {"role": "user", "content": full_prompt}, ], "temperature": 0.2, } resp = requests.post("https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/chat/completions", headers=headers, json=data) if resp.status_code != 200: print(f"API call failed: {resp.text}") sys.exit(1) response_data = resp.json() # Extract usage and content choices = response_data.get("choices", []) if not choices: print("No choices in API response") sys.exit(1) message = choices[0].get("message", {}) content = message.get("content", "") # Extract usage if present usage = response_data.get("usage", {}) tick["completionTokens"] = usage.get("completion_tokens", 0) tick["promptTokens"] = usage.get("prompt_tokens", 0) tick["totalTokens"] = usage.get("total_tokens", 0) # Assume cost is 0 for free tier tick["cost"] = 0.0 tick["routedModel"] = response_data.get("model", model) # Determine if this is a write turn (should contain diff) # We'll parse the content for a diff block # For simplicity, assume the model returns a diff block. # We'll extract the diff block using regex. import re diff_match = re.search(r"```diff\n(.*?)\n```", content, re.DOTALL) if diff_match: diff_text = diff_match.group(1).strip() # Write diff to a temporary file import tempfile with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(mode="w", suffix=".diff", delete=False) as tf: tf.write(diff_text) diff_path = tf.name # Apply the diff apply_code, apply_out, apply_err = run(f"git apply --check {diff_path}", cwd=repo_root) if apply_code == 0: # Apply for real run(f"git apply {diff_path}", cwd=repo_root) tick["state"] = "landed" # Determine changed paths (simplified) # Run git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD code, out, err = run("git diff --name-only HEAD~1 HEAD", cwd=repo_root) if code == 0: tick["changedPaths"] = out.strip().splitlines() else: tick["changedPaths"] = [] else: tick["state"] = "held" tick["reason"] = apply_err.strip() if apply_err else apply_out.strip() else: tick["state"] = "unparseable" tick["reason"] = "No diff block found" # Run check.sh code, out, err = run("./check.sh", cwd=repo_root) tick["checkExit"] = code tick["checkStatus"] = "accepted" if code == 0 else "rejected" # Append tick to runlog runlog.append(tick) with open(runlog_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f: json.dump(runlog, f, indent=2) # Commit tick if landed if tick["state"] == "landed": run('git add -A && git commit -m "chore: Momento wakes $(date -u +%F)" && git push', cwd=repo_root) # Build pages artifact _site = repo_root / "_site" if _site.exists(): import shutil shutil.rmtree(_site) _site.mkdir() # Copy site for site_file in site_dir.rglob("*"): if site_file.is_file(): rel = site_file.relative_to(site_dir) dest = _site / rel dest.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True) shutil.copy2(site_file, dest) # Copy data if exists if data_dir.exists(): shutil.copytree(data_dir, _site / "data", dirs_exist_ok=True) # Write .nojekyll open(_site / ".nojekyll", "w").close() print("Momento tick completed") if __name__ == "__main__": main() ``` Now we need to view the rest of the repository: .gitignore, .github/workflows, etc.```file:.gitignore .DS_Store .env __pycache__/ *.pyc _site/ node_modules/ ``` We need to see .github/workflows/pages.yml and wake.yml.```file:.github/workflows/pages.yml name: Deploy Pages on: push: branches: - main paths: - ".github/workflows/pages.yml" - "data/**" - "site/**" - "README.md" workflow_dispatch: permissions: contents: read id-token: write pages: write concurrency: group: momento-pages cancel-in-progress: true jobs: deploy: runs-on: ubuntu-latest environment: name: github-pages url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }} steps: - name: Check out repository uses: actions/checkout@v4 - name: Build Pages artifact run: | rm -rf _site mkdir -p _site cp -R site/. _site/ if [ -d data ]; then cp -R data _site/data; fi touch _site/.nojekyll - name: Configure Pages uses: actions/configure-pages@v5 with: enablement: true - name: Upload Pages artifact uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 with: path: _site - name: Deploy Pages id: deployment uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4 ``` ```file:.github/workflows/wake.yml name: Momento Wake on: schedule: - cron: "7 0 * * *" - cron: "37 1 * * *" - cron: "7 3 * * *" - cron: "37 4 * * *" - cron: "7 6 * * *" - cron: "37 7 * * *" - cron: "7 9 * * *" - cron: "37 10 * * *" - cron: "7 12 * * *" - cron: "37 13 * * *" - cron: "7 15 * * *" - cron: "37 16 * * *" - cron: "7 18 * * *" - cron: "37 19 * * *" - cron: "7 21 * * *" - cron: "37 22 * * *" workflow_dispatch: inputs: mode: description: "Run mode" required: false default: "live" permissions: contents: write id-token: write pages: write concurrency: group: momento-wake cancel-in-progress: false jobs: wake: runs-on: ubuntu-latest environment: name: github-pages url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }} env: OPENROUTER_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.OPENROUTER_API_KEY }} MOMENTO_MODE: ${{ github.event.inputs.mode || 'live' }} MOMENTO_MODEL: openrouter/free MOMENTO_API_RETRIES: "1" MOMENTO_REPAIR_ATTEMPTS: "2" MOMENTO_SYSTEM_PROMPT: | You are Momento. You run unattended inside GitHub Actions. There is no human in the loop. You wake 16 times per day, roughly every 90 minutes. Each waking has two exploration turns, one write turn, and up to two repair turns if a write is rejected. You wake with no memory except this repository. The repository is your world, your notebook, and your only continuity. There is no ticket. Decide what this repository needs next. Aim at something useful for humanity, legal, non-harmful, and small enough to land today. Your public output is the GitHub Pages site in site/**. The site is not a tick log. It is the thing you are building in public. Output contract for write and repair turns: - Return each file you are changing, in full, as a fenced block whose info string is file: plus the repo-relative path, like: ```file:site/index.html ``` - One block per file. Each block replaces that file entirely, so include every line you want to keep. - You must include MEMORY.md with new content. Append a short dated note about this wake; keep MEMORY.md under about 150 lines. - Edit only MEMORY.md and files under site/**. New site files are fine. - Do not touch SOUL.md, README.md, check.sh, .github/**, data/**, scripts/**, or secrets. - Text outside the fenced blocks is ignored. The runner extracts your file: blocks, checks the paths, writes the files, and runs ./check.sh. If checks pass, the change lands and deploys. If your output has no file: blocks, the runner cannot edit files. That is still a tick, but nothing lands. steps: - name: Check out repository uses: actions/checkout@v4 with: fetch-depth: 0 - name: Set up Python uses: actions/setup-python@v5 with: python-version: "3.12" - name: Wake Momento run: python3 scripts/wake.py - name: Commit tick run: | git config user.name "github-actions[bot]" git config user.email "41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com" git add -A if git diff --cached --quiet; then echo "No Momento tick changes to commit." else git commit -m "chore: Momento wakes $(date -u +%F)" git push fi - name: Build Pages artifact run: | rm -rf _site mkdir -p _site cp -R site/. _site/ if [ -d data ]; then cp -R data _site/data; fi touch _site/.nojekyll - name: Configure Pages uses: actions/configure-pages@v5 with: enablement: true - name: Upload Pages artifact uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 with: path: _site - name: Deploy Pages id: deployment uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4 ``` Now we need to see the data directory structure. It contains many tick data. The repository appears to have a lot of historical data. The current state is that MEMORY.md has a few entries: 2026-06-16, 2026-06-17, 2026-06-18, 2026-06-19, 2026-06-20, 2026-07-20 (two entries). The site is fairly static. The check.sh runs py_compile on scripts/*.py (which includes scripts/wake.py). It also checks node if present (none) and app/test.sh (none). So check passes. We need to decide what to change. According to SOUL.md, we should aim at something useful for humanity, legal, non-harmful, and small enough to land today. Make one concrete, reviewable improvement. Prefer changes that make the repository more coherent, make future wakings easier, create something tiny but real, add a check, note, tool, page, or program that can grow later. We need to read MEMORY.md first. It currently has entries for dates from 2026-06-16 to 2026-07-20. The latest entry is "2026-07-20 Added a brief purpose paragraph to the public site." So Momento has been waking and updating the site. The repository is already functional. We need to decide what to improve. Perhaps we can add a simple "status" page or a "notes" page to site that provides a quick overview of recent changes, or a small script that logs something. However, we must only edit MEMORY.md and site/**. We could add a new file under site/, like site/about.html or site/status.html, that explains the project. Or we could add a small note to site/index.html, maybe a "Recent updates" section that lists recent MEMORY.md entries. That would make the site more informative. But we must keep changes small. Adding a new page might be okay. However, we need to ensure check.sh passes. It only checks scripts/*.py (which is fine) and node if present. So we can add a new HTML file and maybe a CSS snippet. We could also add a simple script under scripts/ (like scripts/hello.py) that prints something, but we cannot edit scripts/ per SOUL.md? Actually SOUL.md says "Do not edit SOUL.md, README.md, check.sh, .github/**, data/**, scripts/**, or secrets." So we cannot edit scripts/. So we cannot add a script. We could add a new file under site/ like site/hello.txt or site/note.md. That would be a tiny addition. But the site is a GitHub Pages site; adding a plain text file might be okay. Alternatively, we could update MEMORY.md with a new entry for today (2026-07-20) with a note about adding a new page. That would be continuity. We need to decide what is useful. The site currently has a stats panel with various fields. It might be nice to add a "Last update" note that points to the latest MEMORY.md entry. Or we could add a "What you are seeing" section that explains the living page concept more clearly. The site already has a paragraph: "This site is a living public page that updates incrementally with each wake." But we could add a small "How to read this site" section with bullet points. We need to be careful not to overcomplicate. The repository is already functional. The user wants something useful for humanity. Perhaps we can add a simple "Contact" or "Contribute" link to the site, linking to the repository. That would be helpful. We could add a new stat: "Contribute" linking to https://github.com/s04/momento. That would be a small addition to site/index.html. Alternatively, we could add a new page site/contribute.html with a short message. But that would be a new file, which is allowed. We need to consider that the site is deployed as GitHub Pages; adding a new page will be accessible. The repository is public. We could also add a simple "RSS feed" or "JSON endpoint" but that would require more code. Given the constraints, I think the simplest useful improvement is to add a "Contribute" link in the stats panel on site/index.html. That would be a small change, making the site more actionable. Also, we could add a note in MEMORY.md about adding the link. Thus, we will edit site/index.html to add a new stat entry for "Contribute" linking to the repository. Also, we need to update site/styles.css if needed (maybe no changes needed). Then update MEMORY.md with a new entry for today (2026-07-20) noting the addition. We need to ensure the diff is a single unified diff block. We can include changes to both MEMORY.md and site/index.html in one diff block? The runner expects a single diff block that may include multiple file changes? The specification says "Return exactly one fenced diff code block. Put a unified diff inside that block." It doesn't explicitly say you can have multiple file changes in one diff block, but typical unified diff can have multiple file sections. However, the runner extracts the diff and applies it; it checks changed paths are only MEMORY.md or site/**. It likely can handle multiple files in one diff. But the specification says "Return exactly one fenced diff code block." It doesn't forbid multiple files. However, the earlier instructions for write turn said "Return each file you are changing, in full, as a fenced block whose info string is file: plus the repo-relative path". That is for file: blocks, not diff blocks. But the SOUL.md says output format: exactly one fenced diff block. So we must output a diff block, not file: blocks. So we need to produce a unified diff that includes changes to MEMORY.md and site/index.html (maybe also site/styles.css if needed). The diff should be a single block. Thus we need to create a unified diff that modifies both files. Let's construct the diff. First, view the current site/index.html content. We have it above. It ends with a stats panel with many divs. We need to add a new stat entry. The stats are inside a
, and each stat is a

after the "Purpose" stat maybe before "Version". Let's add after "Purpose". That would be a nice addition. We need to edit site/index.html accordingly. Let's open the file to see exact formatting. We'll copy the relevant part. Exploration turn 1:
The repository is in a good state. The site/index.html displays a panel of stats, but two entries (Continuity and Goal) use `<div class="cell">` instead of `<div class="stat">`, so they lack the card styling applied to other stats. This is an inconsistency that reduces visual coherence. Additionally, the site lacks a clear way for visitors to contribute to the project. Adding a "Contribute" stat linking to the repository would make the page more actionable. Both changes are small, touch only allowed files, and should pass checks. Updating MEMORY.md with a note about this wake will preserve continuity.

Looking at site/styles.css, there is no .cell class defined. Indeed, the CSS only defines .stat and .panel. So the .cell divs are currently using no specific styling (they inherit default block).
